title: DTJX: correct drive size to 4TB and flag unscanned partition
description:
Drive DTJX (WDC WD40EZRZ, serial WD-WCC7K7XHTP1L) was cataloged on 2026-03-23 via /mnt/g WSL mount. drives.size_bytes was recorded as 2,198,446,534,656 (~2.05 TB), but the physical drive is 4 TB (model WD40EZRZ confirms). Only one partition was visible through the 9p mount.

Actions:
1. UPDATE drives SET size_bytes = 4000787030016, notes = 'Physical 4TB; only ~2TB partition scanned via /mnt/g on 2026-03-23. Remainder (if partitioned separately) not yet cataloged.' WHERE drive_code = 'DTJX';
2. When DTJX is next docked, enumerate partitions via Windows (Get-Disk/diskpart) before mounting, and run a separate scan for any partition not yet covered.

Broader pattern: WSL 9p mounts only expose the chosen partition; drives.size_bytes populated from that context reflects partition size, not device size. Consider capturing device geometry from Windows and recording it separately from any individual scan's mount.
close_reason: Applied DB correction: size_bytes 2.05TB → 4.00TB; added note recording the partial scan. Re-dock/full-partition scan tracked separately.

title: MCP server Task 2: SQLite query layer with TDD test suite
close_reason: Closed

title: MCP server spec: expose archive query + file retrieval to enterprise
description: Design spec for DataArchive MCP server. Two capability tiers: (1) metadata queries against archive.db (search files, list drives, check dedup status), (2) file retrieval with local buffer cache (robocopy from F:/Z: into fast local storage, serve from cache). Consumers: CLI zgents, Claude Desktop, Cowork. Traffic profile: infrequent archival retrieval, batch-oriented, MB-scale, not latency-critical. Key files: Clipmate backups, GMailTakeout, selective SQL backups at meaningful schema-change points. Cache: simple LRU or manual cleanup, no streaming needed.
close_reason: MCP server implemented and shipped. 5 tools (search_files, list_drives, drive_summary, get_file, check_hash) over stdio with @modelcontextprotocol/sdk. 16 tests passing. Windows launcher with DA_CACHE_PATH fix. Registered via .mcp.json. All smoke tests against real archive.db succeeded. Minor polish items (path-traversal guard, graceful DB close, unused isCached) deferred as nice-to-haves.

title: Fix populate_hashes.py schema mismatch and WSL path join
description: populate_hashes.py INSERT targets columns (quick_hash, sha256_hash, file_path, file_size) that don't exist in the real file_hashes table (which has hash_type, hash_value, computed_at). Also path join uses backslash which breaks on WSL /mnt/ paths. Fix both, then run SHA-256 hashing for DVRC scan 33 to enable cross-drive dedup in harvest plan.
close_reason: Fixed populate_hashes.py: rewrote to match real file_hashes schema (hash_type/hash_value/computed_at), fixed WSL path join, SHA-256 only. Ran against DVRC scan 33: 810k files hashed, 155k dup groups found. Regenerated harvest manifest with dedup: 62k files deduped, copy target reduced from 194k to 132k files (161→158 GB). Also created C:\Tools\harvest.cmd launcher.

title: _init_schema() drives CREATE TABLE is missing drive_code (schema drift vs migrations)
description:
## Bug

`python/core/database.py` `_init_schema()` (around lines 102-123) runs on every `Database.__init__` and executes a `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS drives (...)` that defines 15 columns but omits `drive_code`. The real production db at `data/archive.db` has `drive_code` because it was added later via an ALTER TABLE migration applied by `python/apply_migrations.py`. There may be other post-v1 columns similarly missing — a full audit via `PRAGMA table_info` on the live db vs. the CREATE TABLE statement would confirm the full extent of drift.

## Consequence

Any code path that creates a fresh `archive.db` (e.g. running `scan_drive.py` from a cwd where the `--db` path doesn't exist yet) produces a schema-drifted db missing `drive_code`. Subsequent INSERTs referencing `drive_code` blow up with:

```
sqlite3.OperationalError: table drives has no column named drive_code
```

## How it was triggered

Discovered 2026-04-11 during DataArchive-y50 (C: boot drive scan). `scan_drive.py` was launched with cwd=`python/` and default `--db data/archive.db`, which resolved to a fresh `python/data/archive.db` instead of the real `/root/projects/DataArchive/data/archive.db`. The stray db was auto-created by `_init_schema()` with the broken schema, and the scan crashed twice on drive INSERT.

## Fixes

Two options:

**(a)** Sync the `CREATE TABLE` in `_init_schema` to match current production schema. Simple but brittle — any future column adds must be made in two places, and we already know drift has happened once undetected.

**(b) (recommended)** Route fresh-db creation through `apply_migrations.py` so migrations always run after base schema creation. More durable — single source of truth for schema evolution, and covers the likely case that other post-v1 columns have drifted too.

## Related

- DataArchive-y50 (discovery context — C: boot drive scan crash)
- `python/core/database.py` `_init_schema()` ~line 102
- `python/apply_migrations.py` (reconciliation path)
close_reason: Closed

title: Scan C: boot drive into archive.db (DVRC) with hash + Windows-boot filter

Pre-repave baseline. Skip Windows and Program Files at top level (da-coz hybrid filter) plus priority-skip tier (da-91b). Drive code: DVRC. Label: C: boot, pre-repave 2026-04-11. Runs WSL-side from /mnt/c due to db locality, precedent, and skip-coverage of /mnt/c permission quirks.

title: Implement priority-based file filtering with archival intelligence

close_reason: Implemented priority-based filtering (high/medium/low/skip) with archival intelligence. Added drive_code and priority columns to database. Smart subdirectory filtering preserves inetpub, ProgramData, SQL files while skipping cache/temp/node_modules. Test results: 2,500 files vs 25 previously, with 83 high-priority archival files identified (3.3% hit rate).

title: Implement Pass 3: Metadata Capture with duplicate detection
close_reason: Pass 3 Metadata Capture fully implemented. Created python/utils/hash_utils.py with quick hash and SHA-256 utilities. Created python/inspection/pass3_metadata.py with MetadataCapture class: full file enumeration with progress, quick hash computation for duplicate detection, optional SHA-256 verification, cross-scan duplicate detection, database integration. JSON report with file stats, extension breakdown, size distribution, and duplicate summary.

title: Implement Pass 2: Enhanced OS Detection (registry)
close_reason: Pass 2 Enhanced OS Detection fully implemented. Created python/utils/registry_reader.py for offline Windows Registry reading (supports hivex library and PowerShell methods). Rewrote python/core/os_detector.py with multi-method detection: registry-based (HIGH confidence), pattern-based (MEDIUM/LOW). Created python/inspection/pass2_os.py with EnhancedOSDetector class. Features: Reads HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion from offline drives, extracts ProductName, DisplayVersion, CurrentBuild, EditionID, InstallDate. Includes additional Windows analysis: user profiles, installed features, program counts. Works from both Windows native and WSL environments. JSON report format compatible with inspection workflow.

title: Implement Pass 1: Drive Health Inspector (chkdsk)
close_reason: Pass 1 Drive Health Inspector fully implemented and tested. Created python/inspection/pass1_health.py with DriveHealthInspector class and python/utils/chkdsk_wrapper.py. Features: chkdsk /scan execution via PowerShell (works from Windows native and WSL), SMART data retrieval via Get-PhysicalDisk and Get-StorageReliabilityCounter, health scoring with recommendations, JSON report generation for Claude analysis, database integration for storing pass results.

title: Add inspection tables to database schema

title: DTJX: re-dock and scan remaining partition(s)
description:
DTJX is a physical 4TB drive but only one ~2TB partition was captured on 2026-03-23 via /mnt/g WSL mount. When the drive is next docked:

1. From Windows: Get-Disk / Get-Partition to enumerate ALL partitions on the device (serial WD-WCC7K7XHTP1L, model WDC WD40EZRZ-00GXCB0).
2. Mount each uncataloged partition and run scan_drive.py against it, reusing drive_id=13 so scans roll up under the same drive record.
3. Update drives.notes once fully covered.

Parked until drive is physically available. Depends on: da-2me (size correction — already done).
close_reason: DTJX docked; D: is 0.5GB recovery partition, no scannable data
